standard_description:
Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
Grade 3 expectations in this domain are limited to fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

Task
This activity is designed for pairs of students. They will require a set of cards (which are supplied as an attached resource, after the commentary). The goal is to compare the two fractions appearing on each card, determine if they are equivalent and, if not, which is larger. Instructions for the activity are as follows:
Students go through the following steps with the fraction cards:
The pair of students select a card.
Each student individually decides whether the fractions are equal and, if not, which is greater. Then they show each other their choice.
If the partners agree, they take turns explaining their reasoning. If they disagree, they discuss until reaching a consensus.
Repeat 1 through 3 with a new card.
After 10 rounds, each pair records observations about what methods they used to compare the fractions.

khan_exercises.title: Compare fractions with the same numerator
khan_exercises.description: Compare two fractions that have the same numerator using greater and less than symbols.
topic_path: Compare fractions with the same numerator

hints: Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ half is larger than $1$ third.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{2}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ half is larger than $1$ eighth, so $2$ halves is larger than $2$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $5$. So, each whole will have $5$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{5}{6}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $5$ sixths is larger than $5$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{5}{6}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $6$. So, each whole will have $6$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{6}{4}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $6$ fourths is larger than $6$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{6}{4}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{6}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ third is larger than $1$ sixth, so $2$ thirds is larger than $2$ sixths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{3}} > \purple{\dfrac{2}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $4$. So, each whole will have $4$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $4$ sixths is larger than $4$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{6}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is smaller than $1$ fourth.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{6}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]


$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ half, so $3$ eighths is smaller than $3$ halves.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $4$. So, each whole will have $4$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{4}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{3}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is smaller than $1$ third, so $4$ fourths is smaller than $4$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{4}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $7$. So, each whole will have $7$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{7}{8}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ sixth, so $7$ eighths is smaller than $7$ sixths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{7}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $8$. So, each whole will have $8$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{8}{8}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ fourth, so $8$ eighths is smaller than $8$ fourths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{8}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{3}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is smaller than $1$ third, so $3$ fourths is smaller than $3$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{4}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{3}}$

khan_exercises.title: Compare fractions with the same numerator
khan_exercises.description: Compare two fractions that have the same numerator using greater and less than symbols.
topic_path: Module 5: Fractions as numbers on the number line
Topic F: Comparison, order, and size of fractions
Compare fractions with the same numerator

hints: Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ half is larger than $1$ third.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{2}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ half is larger than $1$ eighth, so $2$ halves is larger than $2$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $5$. So, each whole will have $5$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{5}{6}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $5$ sixths is larger than $5$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{5}{6}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $6$. So, each whole will have $6$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{6}{4}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $6$ fourths is larger than $6$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{6}{4}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{6}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ third is larger than $1$ sixth, so $2$ thirds is larger than $2$ sixths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{3}} > \purple{\dfrac{2}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $4$. So, each whole will have $4$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $4$ sixths is larger than $4$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{6}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is smaller than $1$ fourth.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{6}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]


$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ half, so $3$ eighths is smaller than $3$ halves.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $4$. So, each whole will have $4$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{4}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{3}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is smaller than $1$ third, so $4$ fourths is smaller than $4$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{4}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $7$. So, each whole will have $7$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{7}{8}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ sixth, so $7$ eighths is smaller than $7$ sixths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{7}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $8$. So, each whole will have $8$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{8}{8}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ fourth, so $8$ eighths is smaller than $8$ fourths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{8}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{3}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is smaller than $1$ third, so $3$ fourths is smaller than $3$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{4}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{3}}$
